message:
feat(core): event-driven snapshot triggers and trigger channel (wa-3kxe)

Extend SnapshotEngine with event-driven trigger support, enabling the
intelligent snapshot scheduling mode introduced in the config.

SnapshotTrigger — new variants for value-driven scheduling:
- PeriodicFallback: reduced-frequency periodic capture when intelligent
  mode is active (distinct from the full-rate Periodic timer)
- WorkCompleted: agent completed significant work (high capture value)
- HazardThreshold: hazard estimate crossed safety threshold
- StateTransition: agent state machine transition detected
- IdleWindow: extended idle period before potential restart
- MemoryPressure: memory pressure increased, worth snapshotting before
  potential OOM-driven eviction

SnapshotEngine — trigger channel:
- New `trigger_tx: mpsc::Sender<SnapshotTrigger>` and `trigger_rx` fields
  providing an internal channel for external components to inject
  event-driven capture requests
- `emit_trigger()` method: non-blocking try_send that returns false
  if the 512-slot trigger queue is full or no receiver is active
- Updated state-hash dedup check to treat PeriodicFallback identically
  to Periodic (skip if unchanged), while all event-driven triggers
  capture unconditionally regardless of state hash

All new trigger variants map to "event" in the database trigger_type
column, while PeriodicFallback maps to "periodic" alongside Periodic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
